conversation.text_en: Hi, I'm your therapist. Everything we discuss here is confidential, so please feel comfortable.
Thanks, I appreciate that.
Sorry to hear that. What did you do to manage the stress of dealing with that traffic?
Honestly, I just got home and collapsed on the couch. Watched some TV, maybe got a little lost in the show.
That sounds like a nice way to unwind. Do you have any hobbies or activities that bring you joy?
Yeah, I mean, I love working out with my clients at the gym. It's what gets me going in the morning.
That's great! You mentioned earlier that traffic was a nightmare. Did it feel like something specific triggered your stress levels?
Nah, just the usual Friday afternoon commute chaos. But, you know, I did miss a workout class this week because I was so drained after work.
Sorry to hear that. What's been going on at work that might be contributing to those feelings of exhaustion?
Just trying to keep up with demand and meet the expectations of my boss. It's like, no matter how much I do, it's never enough, you know?
That sounds frustrating. How have your relationships with friends and family been lately?
They're okay, I guess. I mean, we don't really hang out as much since work has gotten so crazy.
It can be tough to balance work and personal life. Have you found yourself avoiding social situations or plans recently?
Yeah, actually. Last night was supposed to be a friends' game night, but I ended up bailing at the last minute because I just didn't feel like putting on a happy face.
That can be really tough. What do you think is driving that avoidance behavior?
Honestly, I don't know. Maybe it's just easier to hide out and not deal with everything.
It sounds like there might be some feelings of guilt or shame tied up in that avoidance. Can you tell me more about what's been going on for you?
Guilt? Um, yeah... maybe. I don't know if it's justified, but sometimes I feel like I'm letting people down by not being there.
That can be a heavy burden to carry. Have you found yourself feeling guilty or responsible for things that aren't necessarily your fault?
Yeah, definitely. Like when my boss calls me out for missing a deadline and I just feel like I've failed again.
It sounds like there might be some negative self-talk going on. Can you tell me more about what that's like for you?
Yeah... it's like this constant nagging voice in the back of my head, telling me I'm not good enough or competent enough.
That can be really tough to deal with. Have you tried to challenge those negative thoughts or reframe them in a more positive light?
Honestly? No. It just seems too overwhelming to even try.
That's okay. Sometimes it's hard to know where to start. What do you think would make things easier for you right now?
Maybe just knowing that someone is listening and doesn't judge me, I guess.
You're in a safe space here. I'm listening without judgment. Can we talk about your appetite lately? Have you noticed any changes?
Yeah... I've been eating more out of habit than actual hunger, to be honest.
That can be tough. What do you think is driving that change in your eating habits?
Maybe just trying to cope with everything else going on. Like, if I'm not feeling good enough, maybe I won't feel as bad about stuffing my face.
It sounds like there might be some emotional eating going on. Can we explore what's behind that for you?
Yeah... it's hard to put into words, but sometimes I just need something to make me feel better, you know?
Yeah, I think many people can relate to that. What are your goals for our time together today? Is there anything specific you'd like to work on or achieve?
Honestly? Just feeling less stuck, maybe figuring out what's going on and why I'm so miserable all the time.
That sounds like a great starting point. We can definitely explore those questions further.
